---
title: "About Me"
---

Data & Policy Analytics Leader \| K-12 Education Strategy \| Causal Inference, Forecasting & Enrollment Modeling \| Grants & Financial Stewardship \| Data Governance \| Program Evaluation \| Research Partnerships

Data and policy leader with 10+ years of experience using analytics, causal inference, and strategic planning to support decision-making in large K-12 school systems.  

My work sits at the intersection of education policy, finance, data science, and operational strategy. I translate complex administrative data into insights that help districts allocate resources, expand program access, strengthen compliance, and improve student outcomes.  

At Houston ISD, I have led analytics initiatives supporting enrollment strategy, pre-K expansion, staffing models, capital planning, grant implementation, and research partnerships. My work has helped district leaders make decisions affecting hundreds of schools, millions of student records, and major public investments.  

**Selected impact includes:** • Led enrollment and retention modeling that preserved \$65.7M in state funding by identifying strategies to retain 4,570 students. • Directed analytics informing pre-K expansion across 160+ campuses, increasing classroom capacity and improving access for families. • Supported a RAND randomized controlled trial of Zearn Math across 32,000+ students and 845 teachers. • Managed \$62.3M in federal grant programs with compliant reporting and zero audit findings. • Built reproducible analytics pipelines and decision dashboards used by district leadership for staffing, program evaluation, and capital planning.  

My background combines advanced statistical analysis, program evaluation, financial management, and policy implementation. I regularly work with large administrative datasets covering millions of student records and apply methods such as clustering, simulation modeling, causal inference, and forecasting.  

I have also authored multiple program evaluations and research publications focused on STEM education, student outcomes, and teacher incentives, and have collaborated with universities and national research organizations.  

I am motivated by a simple goal: use rigorous data analysis and thoughtful questions to help education systems make better strategic decisions that expand opportunity and improve outcomes for students.
